commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix flaky parallel tool dispatch test on CI

Pre-warm ThreadPool and increase sleep from 120ms to 500ms so
concurrent execution is observed even on contended CI runners.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pass outputDimension for VoyageAI embeddings (#243)

* fix: pass outputDimension for VoyageAI embeddings

The PrismGateway maps the dimensions parameter to provider-specific
options for Gemini and OpenAI but not for VoyageAI. This causes
VoyageAI to return its default 1024-dimension vectors instead of
the requested size, leading to dimension mismatches when storing
in pgvector columns configured for larger dimensions.

Prism already supports `outputDimension` as a provider option for
VoyageAI (prism-php/prism#904), so we just need to pass it through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: verify provider-specific dimension options in PrismGateway

Adds unit tests verifying that PrismGateway::generateEmbeddings()
passes the correct provider option key for embedding dimensions:
- OpenAI: dimensions
- Gemini: outputDimensionality
- VoyageAI: outputDimension

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Delete tests/Unit/Gateway/Prism/PrismEmbeddingsTest.php

---------

commit.message:
Fix cluster lookup to use UniProt accessions, use file 5 for structural clusters

- Cluster files use bare UniProt accessions, not AFDB entry IDs
  (AF-XXX-F1) — manifest enrichment now joins on uniprotAccession
- Use file 5 (allmembers, cluFlag=2) instead of file 1 for structural
  clusters — same 30M members, but file 5 is the canonical source
- Structural cluster loader filters to cluFlag=2 only
- Verified: every AFDB50 seq cluster with structural members maps to
  exactly one structural cluster — zero cross-cluster leakage possible

commit.message:
fix: make pyarrow imports lazy to fix CI

pyarrow was imported at module level but not declared as a dependency
in pyproject.toml. This caused pytest collection to crash with
ModuleNotFoundError in CI, preventing all tests from running.

Move pyarrow imports to function/method level so modules can be
imported without pyarrow installed. pyarrow is only needed at runtime
when backtest functions are actually called.

commit.message:
Dynamic reducers — builder pattern, DynamicCtx, runtime-flexible access (#23)

* feat: add DynamicResolved — pre-resolved component lookup for dynamic reducers

Sorted-by-TypeId entries with O(log n) binary search at runtime.
Tracks Access metadata and spawn bundle declarations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add DynamicCtx — runtime context for dynamic reducer closures

read/try_read for live component access, write/try_write for buffered
mutations via EnumChangeSet, spawn for entity creation. All operations
gated by DynamicResolved declarations with debug assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: add DynamicReducerId, ReducerSlot, DynamicReducerEntry

Introduce ReducerSlot enum to discriminate unified vs dynamic reducers
in the by_name lookup table. Add DynamicReducerEntry and DynamicAdapter
type alias for type-erased dynamic closures. All existing tests pass
unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add DynamicReducerBuilder — builder pattern for dynamic registration

Builder API: can_read<T>(), can_write<T>(), can_spawn<B>(), build(closure).
Registers components, builds Access bitsets, constructs DynamicResolved,
type-erases the closure, and stores in the dynamic_reducers vec.

Also derives Clone on Access to support cloning into DynamicResolved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add dynamic dispatch — dynamic_call, dynamic_id_by_name, dynamic_access

dynamic_call runs the closure inside strategy.transact() with a DynamicCtx
constructed from Tx::reducer_parts(). dynamic_id_by_name provides typed
lookup discriminated from unified/query reducers. dynamic_access exposes
the builder-declared Access bitset for scheduler conflict analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: debug assertion tests + public exports for dynamic reducers

Add tests verifying that debug_assert catches:
- writing a component declared as read-only
- spawning with an undeclared bundle type

Export DynamicCtx, DynamicReducerBuilder, DynamicReducerId from lib.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(reducer): review fixes — panic message, dedup assert, missing tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(examples): dynamic reducer demo — conditional access, name lookup, conflict detection

* docs: add dynamic reducer types to CLAUDE.md — execution model, pub list, dispatch methods

* fix(reducer): promote debug_assert to assert for write/spawn access checks + dedup invariant

Review feedback: debug_assert on write-to-read-only and spawn-undeclared
allowed silent access violation in release builds, breaking conflict
detection soundness. Now always-on — cost is one bitset lookup per
write/spawn, negligible vs changeset buffering.

Also removes duplicate Access storage from DynamicReducerEntry —
dynamic_access() now delegates to entry.resolved.access().

* test(reducer): review suggestions — undeclared try_read/try_write panics, reverse name collision, spawn abort orphan lifecycle

Also improves duplicate-name panic messages to include slot kind
(unified vs dynamic) for easier debugging.

* docs: add assert boundary rule — assert! when check protects scheduler Access invariants

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: switch reqwest to rustls-tls, removing hidden OpenSSL dependency

reqwest without default-features=false silently pulls in native-tls
(OpenSSL on Linux), contradicting the fully-PQC, no-system-crypto stack.
Switching to rustls-tls makes cross-compilation from macOS work without
OPENSSL_DIR hacks and keeps the entire binary dependency chain in pure Rust.

commit.message:
Fix LoRAUnlockCallback: update scheduler.base_lrs to actually unlock LoRA LR

Bug: LambdaLR computes lr = base_lr * lambda. base_lr=0 at init means
scheduler overrides callback every step → LoRA LR stayed 0 all 193 epochs.

Fix: also set scheduler.base_lrs[idx] and initial_lr so LambdaLR uses
the correct base when computing the cosine schedule after unlock.
Also increase SLURM time 24h → 48h.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Google Chat integration for scheduled deliveries (frontend) (#20886) * feat: add Google Chat integration for scheduled deliveries (backend) Add webhook-based Google Chat support for scheduled deliveries and alerts, following the same pattern as MS Teams. Includes: - Common types: SchedulerGoogleChatTarget, type guards, error class, task payloads - Database: migration for scheduler_google_chat_target table - GoogleChatClient: sends messages using cardsV2 format with image/CSV support - SchedulerModel: CRUD for Google Chat targets - SchedulerClient/Task/Worker: batch notification job processing - Config: GOOGLE_CHAT_ENABLED env var feature flag (default: false) - Analytics: googlechat tracking events Closes #18267 Ref: PROD-947 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * feat: add Google Chat integration for scheduled deliveries (frontend) Add frontend UI for Google Chat webhook-based scheduled deliveries: - Google Chat SVG icon - SchedulerFormGoogleChatInput component (webhook URL tag input) - Form state: googleChatTargets in schedulerFormContext - Conditional rendering based on hasGoogleChat health flag - DestinationFilter: googlechat option in scheduler/log filters - SchedulersTable: Google Chat target display with icon - SchedulersViewUtils: googlechatbatch task name formatting - HealthService: expose hasGoogleChat from GOOGLE_CHAT_ENABLED config Stacked on: feature/google-chat-integration-backend Ref: PROD-947 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: use HTML links in Google Chat footer and replace SVG icon - Footer was using markdown [text](url) but Google Chat textParagraph uses HTML, so links weren't rendering. Changed to text - Replaced placeholder SVG with proper Google Chat icon Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: enable Send Now button when Google Chat targets are present The disabled check for Send Now only considered slack, email, and msTeams targets. Added googleChatTargets to the check. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: make Google Chat image clickable to open full size Add onClick handler to the image widget so users can click to view the full-resolution image in a new tab, matching MS Teams behavior. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * feat: gate Google Chat UI with server feature flag Add GoogleChatEnabled feature flag so the Google Chat input can be enabled per-user via PostHog, not just globally via GOOGLE_CHAT_ENABLED env var. The env var still works as a global override. - Add FeatureFlags.GoogleChatEnabled enum value - Add handler in FeatureFlagModel (env var OR posthog) - Frontend uses useServerFeatureFlag instead of health.hasGoogleChat Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * chore: remove design doc from branch Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: remove env var gate from GoogleChatClient The client threw MissingConfigError if GOOGLE_CHAT_ENABLED wasn't set, breaking users who enable Google Chat via PostHog feature flag. The feature flag controls UI visibility; the client just delivers when called. Also removes hasGoogleChat from the health endpoint since the server feature flag now handles this. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: resolve Google Chat review issues and merge conflict with backend branch - Fix threshold bold formatting bug in GoogleChatClient (inverted HTML tags) - Add missing UpdateSchedulerMsTeamsTarget to UpdateSchedulerAndTargets union - Downgrade webhook payload logging from info to debug level - Add isEmailTarget type narrowing in SchedulersTable (resolves backend branch conflict) - Fix "No destinations" check to include msTeamsTargets Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

feat: monthly_volume_by_version, global TVL by chain, expanded ATH tracking

New data:
- monthly_volume_by_version (V1/V2/V3) from query 22261: sum weeks of current month
- tvl_total by chain + global from query 2617531 (all Balancer versions combined)
  → 8 chains (ethereum, arbitrum, base, polygon, gnosis, avalanche_c, optimism, zkevm)
  → global total: ~$160M

ATH engine expanded (11 → 47 snapshots evaluated per run):
- Added tvl_eth to ATH tracking (was collected but never evaluated)
- Added tvl_total (new) to ATH tracking
- Added weekly_fees, monthly_fees
- Added weekly_swap_volume, monthly_swap_volume
- Added weekly_volume_by_chain, weekly_volume_by_pool_type, weekly_volume_by_version
- Added monthly_volume_by_version, monthly_swaps
- Updated rebuild_aths_from_snapshots SQL to cover all new metric patterns

ATH records: 11 → 46 after this run (first-time initialization for all new metrics)
Real ATH events detected: daily_swap_volume v3 $156M (+15.5%) + daily_swaps 10,489 (+5.3%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: xformers not applicable on CPU-only ComfyUI; better error filtering

- Suppress xformers install action/recommendation on CPU-mode ComfyUI
  deployments (xformers requires CUDA; on CPU it hangs downloading a
  ~200MB binary that provides zero benefit)
- Add --timeout 120 to pip install xformers as a safety net for GPU VMs
- Filter WARNING: and DEPRECATION: lines from stderr so pip metadata
  warnings (e.g. malformed nvidia dist-info) don't trigger false failures
- Handle exit_code == -1 (SSH channel closed without status) explicitly
  with a clear "connection lost or timeout" error instead of treating
  pip warnings as the failure cause
